ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a dedicated Makgeolli entry; 'rice wine' (FOODON_03301710) is the closest defensible parent class. Makgeolli is a specific Korean unfiltered fermented rice beverage within this class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Rice wine) is the closest defensible match. No dedicated Makgeolli code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for Makgeolli. General alcoholic beverage concepts exist but are not sufficiently specific to be defensible here.

allergens: gluten (from nuruk wheat component — varies by producer)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Makgeolli is approximately one glass of 200 ml (approximately 200 g), as commonly poured in Korean restaurants and households.

nutrient_source.title: Makgeolli (Korean rice wine) — HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB Singapore as the data source with confidence 0.95 and review_status 'reviewed'. HPB Foodo lists Makgeolli with the nutrient values preserved in this record. Values are accepted as-is from the reviewed HPB entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — rice-based alcoholic beverages analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Makgeolli exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 54 is estimated by analogy with fermented rice-based beverages and rice wine entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). Makgeolli contains 12.69 g carbohydrate per 100 g; alcohol content (~6–8% ABV) is known to attenuate postprandial glucose response, supporting a low GI estimate. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (54 × 12.69 × 2) / 100 = 13.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Alcohol content (~6–8% ABV) acutely raises blood pressure and interacts with antihypertensive medications; sodium content is low at 24 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Makgeolli entirely, as even moderate alcohol consumption acutely elevates blood pressure and may antagonise antihypertensive medications including ACE inhibitors, ARBs, and beta-blockers.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Residual carbohydrates (12.69 g/100 g) combined with alcohol (~6–8% ABV) cause unpredictable glycaemic fluctuations and can mask hypoglycaemia symptoms
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Makgeolli because its alcohol content can mask hypoglycaemia symptoms — particularly dangerous for those on insulin or sulfonylureas — while its residual carbohydrate load contributes to glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol content (~6–8% ABV) increases cardiovascular risk through elevated triglycerides, arrhythmia risk, and interaction with anticoagulant or antiplatelet medications
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Makgeolli, as regular alcohol consumption raises triglycerides, increases atrial fibrillation risk, and may interact dangerously with anticoagulants such as warfarin and antiplatelet agents.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Alcohol is directly pro-inflammatory and contraindicated with RA medications including methotrexate (hepatotoxicity risk) and biologics
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Makgeolli entirely, as alcohol worsens systemic inflammation and is contraindicated with methotrexate due to additive hepatotoxicity risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Makgeolli (막걸리) is a traditional Korean unfiltered fermented rice wine with a characteristically milky, off-white appearance and a mildly sweet, tangy, and slightly effervescent flavour profile. It is produced by fermenting steamed rice with nuruk — a traditional Korean fermentation starter containing wild yeasts and moulds — and water, resulting in an alcoholic beverage of approximately 6–8% ABV. Unlike filtered rice wines, Makgeolli retains rice sediment, giving it its distinctive cloudy appearance and slightly thick mouthfeel. It is one of Korea's oldest and most widely consumed traditional alcoholic beverages, historically associated with farmers and rural communities, and is now popular across all demographics. Per 100 g, Makgeolli provides approximately 49 kcal, 12.69 g carbohydrate, negligible protein and fat, and 24 mg sodium. Clinically, Makgeolli is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes (unpredictable glycaemic effects from combined alcohol and carbohydrate load), hypertension (alcohol raises blood pressure), cardiovascular disease (alcohol elevates triglycerides and arrhythmia risk), and rheumatoid arthritis (alcohol is pro-inflammatory and contraindicated with methotrexate). It is not halal and is not gluten-free due to potential wheat components in nuruk.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database with review_status 'reviewed' and confidence 0.95. HPB is an approved primary regional source for this record. Korean local-language name confirmed from standard Korean language references. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database peer-reviewed literature, clearly documented.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore database entry for Makgeolli — this fermented rice beverage contains negligible protein as the fermentation process converts most protein substrates; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo. fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore database — Makgeolli contains no fat by composition; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo. sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore database — residual carbohydrates are primarily from unfermented starch fractions, not free sugars as measured; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore database — liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing solids retained after filtration; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for Makgeolli not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo database entry as of 2026. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate based on fermented rice-based beverage entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for Makgeolli exists in approved sources. Alcohol content approximately 6–8% ABV based on standard Makgeolli product specifications; alcohol_g per 100 g not included in per_100g fields as this field is not part of the RESULT template schema.
